title: Koellia lanceolata
intro: Koellia lanceolata is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae, commonly known as the lance-leaf koellia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Koellia lanceolata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Koellia lanceolata is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction to utilize its health benefits, although its use may vary depending on the region and cultural context.
